(CS)²AI Online™ Replay: Cybersecurity of Electric Vehicles & Automations
description:
This is an airing of an event held live in Aug 2025 which was very popular among your fellow CS2AI Community Members. We are replaying it for those of you who missed it at the time.
Register now: [https://events.zoom.us/ev/AqagIqTQxEUzTGXpXiAFj56xxKkv576WtaFyrxMts826Lq7LQZhv~AndaROiOsl4G7QH7Ml1b5bLmimkJXA0R9yDy85oYbDl0YvoU7kEzb3k5_VY6bBhaFB-2YESCA6Y0g0VCsQ35GOaWvg](https://events.zoom.us/ev/AqagIqTQxEUzTGXpXiAFj56xxKkv576WtaFyrxMts826Lq7LQZhv~AndaROiOsl4G7QH7Ml1b5bLmimkJXA0R9yDy85oYbDl0YvoU7kEzb3k5_VY6bBhaFB-2YESCA6Y0g0VCsQ35GOaWvg)

⚡ **Rolling Power: Cybersecurity of Electric Vehicles & Automation** ⚡
As electric vehicles (EVs) and automation rapidly reshape the future of transportation and energy, cybersecurity has become a critical pillar of resilience and trust. From charging infrastructure and connected fleets to autonomous driving systems and smart grids, each innovation brings both opportunity and new attack surfaces.
Join industry leaders, security experts, and innovators as we explore the evolving threat landscape and solutions for safeguarding EVs, their digital ecosystems, and the infrastructure that powers them.
🔑 **Key Topics Include:**

* Securing EV charging networks and supply chains
* Protecting autonomous and connected vehicle systems
* Cyber-physical risks across smart mobility and energy integration
* Building resilience in transportation infrastructure
* Emerging standards, policies, and global collaboration efforts

Whether you’re in automotive, energy, cybersecurity, or policy, this event is your gateway to understanding the challenges and opportunities driving the next era of mobility security.

Click to register now:
[https://events.zoom.us/ev/AqagIqTQxEUzTGXpXiAFj56xxKkv576WtaFyrxMts826Lq7LQZhv~AndaROiOsl4G7QH7Ml1b5bLmimkJXA0R9yDy85oYbDl0YvoU7kEzb3k5_VY6bBhaFB-2YESCA6Y0g0VCsQ35GOaWvg](https://events.zoom.us/ev/AqagIqTQxEUzTGXpXiAFj56xxKkv576WtaFyrxMts826Lq7LQZhv~AndaROiOsl4G7QH7Ml1b5bLmimkJXA0R9yDy85oYbDl0YvoU7kEzb3k5_VY6bBhaFB-2YESCA6Y0g0VCsQ35GOaWvg)

name: OG Coffee&Code Coding!
description:
## ☕ The OG Coffee&Code Meetup

Before the hype. Before the buzzwords. Before “AI-powered everything.”
This is **The OG Code&Coffee** — the meetup that started it all.
No panels. No pitches. No pressure.
Just good people, good coffee, and focused time to **build, learn, and connect**.

### What this meetup is

* An open, welcoming space for **coders, builders, designers, and curious minds**
* A place to **work on your own projects**, homework, startups, side quests, or experiments
* Organic conversations, spontaneous debugging, and genuine community
* Beginner-friendly, expert-respected, ego-free

### What this meetup is *not*

* ❌ A lecture
* ❌ A sales event
* ❌ A recruiting trap
* ❌ A “watch one person talk for 90 minutes” situation

### How it works

* Show up
* Grab coffee ☕
* Open your laptop (or notebook, or brain)
* Work at your own pace
* Chat when you want, focus when you need
* Leave having made progress—or at least a new friend

### Who should come

* Software engineers & data folks
* Students & self-learners
* Founders & indie hackers
* Designers, tinkerers, and technologists
* Anyone who likes building things with other humans

Whether this is your **first Coffee&Code** or you’ve been here since day one—this is the meetup that keeps the spirit alive.
Come for the code.
Stay for the community.
Leave caffeinated ☕🚀

name: SaaS & Tech GROWTH Meetup - Productive Harmony: Manage Energy, Not Your Time
description:
## Come join the industry-leading community for SaaS and Tech growth. Our mission is to build the #1 community supporting SaaS and technology founders, operators, and builders through real relationships, shared learning, and growth-driven collaboration.

​Each Meetup creates intentional networking, actionable insights, and direct access to leaders who understand what it takes to grow, fund, scale, and exit in today’s competitive market.
If you’re serious about building in SaaS or Tech, this is where you belong.

**[REGISTER HERE!](https://luma.com/zylzdxlk)**

## **Agenda**

* ​Networking + Food + Drinks
* ​Welcome & Vision - **Michael C. Bertoni**
* ​**Featured Speaker - RJ Kedziora, Co-Founder of Estenda Solutions**
* ​Host - **Michael Bertoni (CEO at SaaS Talent)**
* ​Live Audience Q&A
* ​Continued Networking + Food + Drinks
* ​Podcast-style interviews in the **VIP Area**

## ​**Featured Speaker - RJ Kedziora**

​Richard "RJ" Kedziora is a proven technology founder and digital health expert who has spent over 30 years designing and deploying high-impact software solutions. He is the co-founder and CEO of Estenda Solutions, a specialized technology agency focusing on custom software development, data analytics, and AI/ML designed to improve patient outcomes, reduce healthcare costs, and seamlessly integrate advanced technology with human-centric care.
​At this Meetup, RJ will introduce the core concepts from his newly released book, *Productive Harmony*, and walk through the real problem it was built to solve: the limitations of traditional time management in a demanding, high-growth world. He’ll share candid lessons from the front lines of building AI-first healthcare solutions, including how founders can shift from maximizing hours to managing energy capacity, how to achieve sustainable performance without burning out, and why the best AI implementations will always prioritize human connection over pure automation.

​Get the book here:
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0GT44LRC1/](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0GT44LRC1/?utm_source=luma)

**Premium Ticket Tiers**

### **Free Ticket**

Access to the Meetup, food, drinks, and networking.
Ideal for SaaS builders and professionals.

### **Silver Ticket**

Includes Free Ticket plus:

* 1 extra drink ticket
* 5-minute podcast-style interview (VIP)
* Edited short- and long-form promo content

### **Gold Ticket**

Includes Silver Ticket plus:

* Private coaching & strategy session with Mike & Liam
* Optional marketing insights with SaaS Talent CMO

## **Hosts & Community**

**​Michael C. Bertoni - Founder & CEO, SaaS Talent**
**Founder, SaaS & Tech GROWTH Meetup**
**[[Connect on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michaelbertoni33/)]**

**Exciting News!**

**​Proudly Recognized as [Technical.ly](https://technical.ly/) Programming of the Year - 2025**

**​**The SaaS & Tech GROWTH Meetup is honored to be named [Technical.ly](https://technical.ly/) Programming of the Year for 2025, recognizing our commitment to building meaningful, high-impact programming for founders, operators, and leaders across the tech ecosystem.

**​New Partner Announcement!**
**​Partner: [Technical.ly](https://technical.ly/)**

**​**Founded in 2009, [Technical.ly](https://technical.ly/) is a multi-local news organization with national reach and a community of 150k+ technologists and entrepreneurs invested in where they live. With a staff of full-time journalists, they report on entrepreneurship, civic tech, AI and jobs of the future, with a focus on innovation and economic development in emerging tech hubs around the US.
​They help innovative professionals - and those who aspire to join them - navigate their careers, connecting and challenging them with stories about entrepreneurship, jobs of the future, civic tech, economic mobility and economies in change.

Local economic development organizations financially underwrite their reporting to ensure their regions become better known for accessible innovation economies. In addition, service providers and tech employers advertise with sponsored content, newsletter marketing and events.
​As a partner, [Technical.ly](https://technical.ly/) supports the mission of the SaaS & Tech Growth Meetup in an effort to bring more connected, In-Real-Life experiences to the Philadelphia region.

**Sponsor:**
**[Duane Morris](https://www.duanemorris.com/)**
**[www.duanemorris.com](https://www.duanemorris.com/)**

Duane Morris LLP, a law firm with more than 900 attorneys in offices across the United States and internationally, provides innovative solutions to today’s legal and business challenges to a broad array of clients. Throughout its more than 120-year history, Duane Morris has fostered a collegial culture, where lawyers collaborate to leverage our collective knowledge and experience. Their lawyers are leaders in a wide range of legal disciplines and are as diverse as the clients they serve. Evolving from a partnership of prominent lawyers in Philadelphia a century ago, Duane Morris' modern organization stretches from the U.S. to Europe and across Asia. While expanding into major markets around the nation and with lawyers practicing around the globe, Duane Morris continues to be an important part of the foundation of the Philadelphia legal community. The city of the firm's founders a century ago is now its present-day headquarters. Hundreds of attorneys from Duane Morris' Philadelphia office work with each other, and with their partners in other cities, to provide a wide array of legal services to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to small entrepreneurial ventures, from public entities to individuals. By sponsoring the SaaS & Tech Growth Meetup, the Firm supports the continued growth of Philadelphia’s tech community. For more information about Duane Morris, please contact [John Neclerio.](https://www.duanemorris.com/attorneys/johnmneclerio.html)

**​Sponsor:**
**SaaS Talent**
**[www.saas-talent.com](https://www.saas-talent.com/)**

**​**Building and growing world class SaaS and Tech Teams by providing GTM, AI, and Engineering talent across the US and Latin America.
10+ years in business · 100+ clients · 50,000+ connections to SaaS Founders

name: Airflow Monthly Virtual Town Hall- April
description:
**PLEASE NOTE- YOU MUST [REGISTER HERE](https://astronomer.zoom.us/meeting/register/3NuEKTdEQfKiBhE1xFgHMg#/registration) TO JOIN**

Join Apache Airflow committers, users, and community leaders for our Monthly Airflow Town Hall! This one-hour event is a collaborative forum to explore new features, discuss AIPs, review the roadmap, and celebrate community highlights.

The Town Hall happens on the first Friday of each month (unless there is a holiday) and will be recorded for those who can't attend. Recordings will be shared on the [Airflow Youtube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/@ApacheAirflow) and posted to the #town-hall channel on [Airflow Slack](https://apache-airflow-slack.herokuapp.com/) and the dev mailing list.

**Agenda**

* Arrivals & Introduction
* Airflow Project Update w/ Jarek Potiuk
* Airflow 3.2.x Release Highlights w/ Rahul Vats
* AIP-105: Pluggable Retry Policies w/ Kaxil Naik
* AIP-102: Business User Interaction w/ Marco Kuettelwesch
* Closing Remarks

**PLEASE NOTE- YOU MUST [REGISTER HERE](https://astronomer.zoom.us/meeting/register/3NuEKTdEQfKiBhE1xFgHMg#/registration) TO JOIN**

name: Debug & Decaf
description:
Where:
**Mad Anthony Wayne Cafe**
**135 N Wayne Ave, Wayne, PA 19087**

When your inbox finally shuts up, we gather for Debug & Decaf... a late-evening meetup for creators who know building in isolation sucks.

Hit a wall? Solved something worth sharing? Need feedback on a new feature?

**What Happens:**

* Grab a cup of coffee, gelato, or snack.
* Share what your stuck on...product decisions, technical challenges, go-to-market questions, whatever's keeping you up at night.
* The group actively debugs together (or separate). Direct feedback, alternative approaches, solutions from different perspectives.

Please make sure to support the cafe through buying a cup of coffee, gelato, or snacks. They've got a lot of good stuff - including their genorsity in hosting us in a cozy spot right off the Wayne train station (super convenient for anyone traveling via SEPTA from the city).

**The Vibe:**
Less networking event in business attire, more late night office hours in sweats. Coffee's flowing. Feedback is direct and helpful. Nobody's selling anything. Open laptops, real conversation, actual problem-solving.
**Dress code:** Super casual. Leave the button-downs at home.

name: SimSense Workshop: Build for any screen
description:
**Use Agents to Build for the Real World with SimSense.**
You know how you ask Claude to build something cool and then... it just lives in the chat? **[SimSense](https://simsense.ai)** gives your Agent a space in the real world. Ask Claude for something, push it to any screen — a tablet, a TV, a browser on your phone, and it lives there. No hosting. No deployment. No Vercel project you'll forget about in a month.
**What to Expect:**

* **Lunch:** Sponsored by SimSense!
* **Guided WorkShop Session:** Come hang out, bring your weirdest ideas, and walk away having built something that's actually running on a screen somewhere with guidance from the SimSense technical team.

**📅 When:** Friday, May 1st, 2026
**🕒 Time:** 12:00PM-2:00PM
**📍 Where:** BlockSpace, 215 Broad Street (5 minute walk from City Hall)
**About the Group:** Our workshops are meant to share knowledge and feedback on tools that companies in our industry are building, and most importantly, build something fun!
**Who Should Join:**

* **Non-Technical People:** If you've tried creating a visualization of something, and want to see it on something other than your own screen, get some hands on guidance from us!
* **Software Engineers:** Explore new tools that make it easier for people at all levels to create their vision and translate it into the real world.

**See you there!**
Links:
Site: [codephilly.com](http://codephilly.com)

name: Composio Saturdays! Let your AI agents orchestrate your many apps in 3 hours!
description:
### 🚀 **Composio Sundays — Build, Share & Connect!**

Welcome to **Composio Sundays**, a relaxed weekly meetup for developers, builders, and AI enthusiasts who are exploring the future of AI agents and integrations with **Composio.dev** — the platform that empowers AI agents and LLMs to interact with real-world tools and workflows.
Whether you’re just getting started or shipping production-ready agents, this event is your space to learn, create, share, and collaborate with others in the ecosystem.
**What to Expect:**
✨ **Show & Tell** – Demo what you’ve built with Composio or tools that work alongside it.
🛠 **Hands-On Sessions** – Pair up to build integrations, troubleshoot toolchains, and explore new agent workflows.
💡 **Knowledge Exchange** – Tips, tricks, and best practices for integrating AI agents into real-world apps.
🤝 **Community Networking** – Meet fellow builders, share ideas, and find collaborators for your next project.
**Who Should Join:**
Developers, architects, product builders, and anyone curious about agentic AI, automation, and practical integrations with platforms like Composio.
**Why Attend:**
Composio makes connecting intelligent agents to external tools simpler and more reliable — and Composio Sundays is your weekly chance to level up with peers. Whether you're automating workflows or crafting production-ready systems, this meetup helps you turn ideas into action.

name: Lakebase for the Lakehouse & an AI-Ready Lakehouse for Precision Diagnostics
description:
# **About this event**

🎉 Our kickoff event!
**Agenda**

* 5:00 – 5:30 PM — Doors open, networking
* 5:30 – 5:40 PM — Welcome & kickoff: organizer introduction and a few words from our Title Sponsor, Lumenalta
* 5:40 – 6:25 PM — Talk 1: *Lakebase: Fully Managed Postgres for the Lakehouse* — Ayman El-Ghazali
* 6:25 – 6:40 PM — Break, food, and mingling
* 6:40 – 7:10 PM — Talk 2: *From Legacy Lab Sprawl to Algorithm* — Jim Havrilla
* 7:10 – 8:00 PM — Networking
* 8:00 PM — Doors close

Food and beverages will be catered.

**RSVP** at https://usergroups.databricks.com/events/details/databricks-user-groups-phillybricks-philadelphia-databricks-user-group-presents-lakebase-for-the-lakehouse-amp-an-ai-ready-lakehouse-for-precision-diagnostics/

**Talk 1 — Lakebase: Fully Managed Postgres for the Lakehouse**
*Ayman El-Ghazali · Senior Solutions Architect, Databricks*
Lakebase is a fully managed, Postgres-compatible OLTP database engine for Databricks that seamlessly supports reverse ETL patterns — syncing curated Lakehouse analytics directly into operational Postgres tables for instant use in applications, AI/BI dashboards, and feature stores.
This architecture lets developers build data-driven apps with millisecond latency and transactional reliability, while also activating ML-powered insights in real time — all without custom pipelines or complicated ETL development.
In this session, Ayman will walk through key capabilities, example use cases, and how Lakebase simplifies infrastructure while unlocking new possibilities for AI and analytics.
**Talk 2 — From Legacy Lab Sprawl to Algorithm: Building an AI-Ready Lakehouse for Precision Diagnostics**
*Jim Havrilla, PhD*
A precision diagnostics lab — specializing in rapid pooled antibiotic susceptibility testing for complicated UTIs — inherited a decade of fragmented data: aging SQL instances, LIS/RCM CSVs, and R&D spreadsheets never meant for analytics.
This talk walks through:

* The decision to build a Medallion-architecture lakehouse on Databricks with Unity Catalog
* The Lakeflow Connect integration that unified Salesforce with lab data
* The patent-pending ML model now predicting antibiotic susceptibility from PCR and demographic inputs ahead of phenotypic results

**About this event**
Welcome to the launch of **PhillyBricks** — the Greater Philadelphia area's first dedicated Databricks User Group, and a practitioner-led community for data engineers, architects, analytics and ML engineers, and platform leaders building on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform.
Philly's tech scene is thriving, with deep benches in fintech, healthcare, life sciences, and enterprise technology — but there has been no dedicated space for Databricks practitioners to go deep on architecture, governance, and real-world implementation. PhillyBricks fills that gap.
For our inaugural meetup, we've lined up two talks that span the full arc of what's possible on Databricks today — from the newest platform capabilities straight from Databricks, to a real-world lakehouse modernization story from a Philadelphia-area precision diagnostics innovator. Come for the talks, stay for the food and conversation, and help us kick off what we hope will be a long-running home for the region's Databricks community.
A huge thank you to our Founding **Title Sponsor, Lumenalta**, for backing this inaugural event, and to **Microsoft** for hosting us at their Malvern office.

**RSVP** at https://usergroups.databricks.com/events/details/databricks-user-groups-phillybricks-philadelphia-databricks-user-group-presents-lakebase-for-the-lakehouse-amp-an-ai-ready-lakehouse-for-precision-diagnostics/

**Important — please read before arriving**

* RSVPs are first-come, first-served. Please cancel if your plans change so we can release your seat to someone on the waitlist.
* **All guests must be on the attendee list for entry.**
* Please bring a **government-issued photo ID** — you'll receive a pre-printed badge upon arrival.
* **Security passes must be returned upon exit.**

**Organized by** Dilorom Abdullah — Databricks MVP \| Databricks Practice Director\, Lumenalta

name: PLUG Central - Jitsi Meet: 7-9PM; Speaker from 7:30-8:30PM
description:
Please join us for PLUG Central. During the pandemic we meet on-line at Jitsi Meet, beginning at 7pm. RSVPs are optional. Anyone interested in Linux and Free and Open Source software is welcome to attend. We welcome all levels of expertise, whether you're just curious, a hobbyist, or a professional.

Jitsi Meet agenda:

Q&A with emcee CJ Fearnley from 7-7:30PM. Bring your Linux (and related) questions for a group exploration.

The main presentation from 7:30-8:30PM: TBD.

Q&A continued from 8:30-9PM.

After the pandemic dissipates, we plan to again meet in Lecture Hall A in Griffith Hall at USP in Philadelphia. We plan to revert to the original speaker slot from 8-9PM with Q&A at the beginning to better support travel constraints.

After the presentation some of us adjourn to Clarkville (http://www.clarkvillephilly.com/) a nearby pizza and craft beer restaurant.

University of the Sciences in Philadelphia (USP)
Griffith Hall (Room "A" or "C", look for the signs)
600 South 43rd Street
Philadelphia, PA 19104-4495

USP is located in University City. Driving directions are available at http://www.phillylinux.org/locations/usp.html, or http://www.usip.edu/directions, both of which have an aerial view of the campus buildings. USP is also easily accessible by public transportation.

Often, the door to Griffith Hall is locked at 7PM. If you find the door locked, please contact the security guard (usually in the booth in the parking lot) and tell them that the monthly PLUG (Philadelphia Linux User Group) meeting in Griffith Hall is tonight. If there is no one in the booth you can call UCS's public safety number at 215.895.1117 and they will radio personnel to unlock the door. Our sponsor is Zhiwei Liu in the Chemistry department. Security knows this and will almost always open the door for you and have it unlocked so other participants can enter without issue.

name: (Co)nnect Philly AI Ecosystem Summit
description:
**Join us for (Co)nnect: Philly's AI Ecosystem Summit, a full-day Philly Tech Week gathering designed to bring together the people building, supporting, and growing AI and tech across Philadelphia.**

The idea behind **(Co)** is simple: **community, collaboration, and connection**.
Philadelphia's AI ecosystem is growing quickly. Founders are building. Researchers are innovating. Operators are applying AI in real business settings. Investors, institutions, and ecosystem leaders are helping shape what comes next. What is often missing is shared space to learn from each other, build relationships, and see the full picture more clearly.
That is what **(Co)nnect** is built to do.

This summit brings together the people shaping AI in Philadelphia for a full day of keynotes, fireside conversations, lightning sessions, demos, and networking at Pennovation Works. The goal is to spotlight the strength, creativity, and momentum already present across Philly's AI and tech scene while creating real opportunities for new connection and collaboration.
Whether you are a founder, builder, researcher, investor, operator, student, or simply someone who wants to better understand where AI in Philadelphia is headed, this event is designed to help you connect with the ecosystem in a meaningful way.

### What to expect

At **(Co)nnect**, you can expect:

* A full-day summit during Philly Tech Week
* A strong mix of ecosystem insight and practical learning
* Keynote conversations on Philadelphia's opportunity in AI and tech
* Fireside chats on capital, builders' journeys, talent, infrastructure, and long-term ecosystem growth
* Lightning sessions and demos from people building in the region
* Networking throughout the day with founders, researchers, investors, operators, and community leaders
* A chance to better understand who is shaping Philly's AI future and where new opportunities are emerging

### Basic agenda

**8:30 AM to 9:30 AM**
Arrival, check-in, light refreshments, and networking
**9:30 AM to 10:25 AM**
Opening welcome and keynote
**10:30 AM to 11:20 AM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round one
**11:30 AM to 12:20 PM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round two
**12:30 PM to 1:30 PM**
Lunch and networking
**1:30 PM to 2:20 PM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round three
**2:30 PM to 3:30 PM**
Closing keynote or panel discussion
**3:30 PM to 5:30 PM**
Happy hour, networking, and close

### Who should attend

This event is for people who are building, backing, studying, or shaping the future of AI and technology in Philadelphia, including:

* Founders and startup teams
* AI builders, developers, and technologists
* Researchers and educators
* Investors and innovation leaders
* Operators and enterprise professionals
* Community builders and ecosystem partners
* Students and curious professionals looking to get more connected to Philly's AI scene

### Why attend

If you want a clearer view of Philadelphia's AI ecosystem, stronger local connections, and more insight into where the region is headed next, this is a room worth being in.
You will leave with:

* A better understanding of the people and organizations shaping AI in Philly
* New relationships across sectors and disciplines
* Fresh perspective on where opportunity is growing
* A stronger sense of how you can plug into the ecosystem

### About the venue

**Pennovation Works** is one of Philadelphia's most exciting hubs for innovation and entrepreneurship, making it a fitting home for a summit centered on community, collaboration, and the future of AI in Philly.

### Speaker note

Speaker announcements are coming soon. We are curating a lineup of founders, builders, researchers, investors, operators, and community leaders who reflect the range and momentum of Philadelphia's AI and tech ecosystem.

### Registration

Space is limited, and advance registration is encouraged.
Come be part of the conversations, ideas, and relationships shaping what comes next for AI in Philadelphia.

name: From PRD to Prototype: Hands-On Vibe Coding for the Product Team
description:
**The Problem We're Solving**
Based on Product Tank’s recent survey, most PMs are stuck at "ChatGPT writes my PRD" - only a handful are actually building anything with AI. There's a massive gap between using AI as a writing assistant and using it to create working prototypes.

This workshop bridges that gap. Attendees will go from a Product Requirements Document to a live prototype they built themselves - in less than 90 minutes.

**What Attendees Will Build**
Attendees will pick a project that adds value to their day-to-day. Below is a list of starter options, but feel free to bring your own idea.

1. **Product landing page** — explain a feature or concept, collect interest (featured/recommended)
2. **Interactive prototype** — a clickable multi-step flow or onboarding sequence
3. **User feedback collector** — submit feedback, tag by theme, see it displayed
4. **Feature voting board** — ranked list of ideas your team can interact with

**Every attendee leaves with:**

* A PRD they’ve developed themselves
* A live website URL they can share
* The actual code and files they can modify later
* Proof they can build working prototypes, not just documents

**Why This Approach Works**

**We're teaching real vibe coding** \- the same professional workflow using Claude Code\- not just drag\-and\-drop tools like Webflow or Wix\. This means:

* **Unlimited creative freedom** \- Build anything you can imagine\, not just pre\-built templates
* **Transferable skills** \- Learn professional skills that work for any project

Key insight: **PMs who can prototype their own ideas move faster and communicate more effectively with engineering teams**. This isn't about becoming a developer - it's about removing the dependency on scarce engineering resources for early validation.

**What Attendees Need**

* Laptop (Mac or Windows)
* Wifi/internet connection
* Claude code for desktop
* No coding experience required.

**Quick note:** This is being held at the Pennovation Center during their [(Co)nnect: Philly AI Ecosystem Summit](https://luma.com/xdwih64h). In addition to registering here, be sure to [also register at this link](https://luma.com/xdwih64h) for building access where you can join other AI-related sessions they have during the day.

**Convince the Boss You Should Go!**
Since this is one of our first ProductTank events during workday hours, we wanted to give you a little help if you need to convince the boss. Here are two, almost ready statements you can use on Slack or Email/when you talk to them!

**Manager-Facing (Email or Direct Ask)**
Hi [Manager], I came across a free 90-minute workshop on May 7 called [From PRD to Prototype: Hands-On Vibe Coding for the Product Team](https://www.meetup.com/producttank-philadelphia/events/314163608/), and I’d like to attend. It’s focused on using AI tools to quickly turn product ideas into working prototypes—not just writing PRDs.

This feels especially relevant as we look to move faster on validation and reduce back-and-forth with engineering on early concepts. The session is very hands-on, and I’d leave with a working prototype plus skills I can immediately apply to our current projects. Since it’s free and relatively short, it seems like a high-value way to build capability in an area that’s becoming increasingly important for PMs.

This is exactly the work we want to be doing, so I’d like to use part of my workday on DATE to attend. I’ll come back with a working prototype and new skills to bring back to the team.

**Short Slack / Quick Message Version**
Hey — there’s a free 90-min workshop on May 7th on using AI to turn PRDs into actual working prototypes. It’s very hands-on, and I’d come back with something built + skills we could use right away to speed up early validation.

This is part of my work day and I’ll get us a lot of value back at the office. Any concerns if I block time to attend?

name: Agentic AI Tools in Action
description:
**[Register here to save your spot!](https://ctrly.io/YFbot )**

**Build, deploy, & scale AI agents in *real* w*orkflows*, not just theory.**

This multi-session webinar blends strategic context, live engineering demos, and applied enterprise use cases to show how agentic workflows are designed, deployed, and operated in the real world.

By the end of the workshop, participants will understand how to architect, build, and evaluate agentic AI workflows that integrate with real tools, services, and infrastructure — skills directly applicable to modern production environments.

**What you'll learn**

* How to design agentic workflows that interact with real tools and APIs
* How AI agents change the way software gets built
* Where agents break—and how to work around limitations
* How to evaluate tools like MindStudio for real-world use
* Patterns for integrating agents into existing systems and processes

**Session Highlights**

2:00 PM ET
**The AI Imperative​: Competitive Pressure Meets Implementation Reality​**
A strategic, high‑level exploration of what this moment truly demands from leaders. This session frames AI as a structural transformation that spans strategy, operating models, governance, and value creation.
*Hosted by: Wade Erickson & Richard Taubin (Motion Consulting Group, a Kelly Company)*

2:15 PM ET
**Modernizing Enterprise Systems with Agentic AI**
Explore how organizations are integrating AI agents into existing systems to automate workflows, reduce manual overhead, and unlock new efficiencies—without starting from scratch.
*Hosted by: Amit Tank (Kelly Science, Engineering, Technology & Telecom)*

2:45 PM ET
**The Future of Programming: Building with AI Agents**
AI agents are rapidly changing how software gets built. This session explores this shift, using Remy by MindStudio as a lens into what programming looks like in an agent-driven world. We’ll cover how agents are reshaping development workflows, where they fall short, and what comes next for builders and teams.
*Hosted by: Dmitry Shapiro (MindStudio)*
